# Decisions: Bicep / Infrastructure as Code
> Stack: bicep-iac | Status: DEFERRED | Applicable: NO
> Deferred-Reason: No Azure infrastructure or Bicep files exist in the codebase. App runs on localhost only (DEC-R2-001). Activate when Azure deployment is added.

---

## Decided Items

| ID | Priority | Scope | Decision | Notes | Date |
|----|-----------|-------|-----------|-------------|-------|
| DEC-102 | HIGH | Phase 2 | Infrastructure must be defined entirely using Infrastructure as Code with Bicep | All Azure resources must be provisioned through Bicep templates stored in the repository. Manual Azure Portal provisioning is prohibited except for break-glass recovery scenarios. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-103 | HIGH | Phase 2 (DevOps) | All infrastructure deployments must run through Azure DevOps pipelines using Bicep | CI/CD pipelines perform what-if validation before deployment and enforce gated approvals for production environments. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-104 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Architecture) | Infrastructure architecture must follow Microsoft Well-Architected Framework principles | Architecture decisions must explicitly address Reliability, Security, Cost Optimization, Operational Excellence, and Performance Efficiency. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-105 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (IaC Structure) | Bicep codebase must use modular template architecture | Core modules stored under /infra/modules and composed by environment-level templates. Modules must be reusable and parameterized. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-106 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Security) | All infrastructure deployments must implement secure defaults aligned with Azure security best practices | Includes managed identities, private endpoints where applicable, RBAC least-privilege roles, and Azure Policy compliance enforcement. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-107 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Reliability) | Infrastructure must include built-in resilience patterns defined in Bicep templates | Where applicable: zone redundancy, automatic scaling, health probes, and retry-friendly architecture patterns. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-108 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Cost Optimization) | All Bicep templates must include cost-aware configuration defaults | Examples include right-sized SKUs, autoscaling policies, lifecycle policies for storage, and cost monitoring integration. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-109 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Operational Excellence) | Infrastructure must integrate Azure Monitor, Log Analytics, and diagnostics at deployment time | Diagnostic settings and monitoring resources must be created automatically via Bicep modules. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-110 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Governance) | Infrastructure must enforce Azure Policy and tagging standards through Bicep deployment | Required tags include environment, owner, cost center, and application. Policy assignments must be deployed alongside resources. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-111 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Environment Strategy) | Infrastructure environments must follow environment parity using parameterized Bicep deployments | Dev, Test, and Prod environments use the same templates with environment-specific parameter files. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-112 | HIGH | Phase 2 (IaC Standards) | Which minimum/target versions are mandated for Bicep, Azure CLI, and deployment tooling, and what is the upgrade cadence? | Pin Bicep and Azure CLI via CI tool manifests/global config; allow patch roll-forward only; review and uplift versions monthly with quarterly minor-version refresh. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-113 | HIGH | Phase 2 (IaC Quality Gates) | Which mandatory pre-deploy checks must pass (bicep build, lint, policy-as-code validation, security scan), and which checks are blocking? | Blocking gates: `bicep build`, `bicep lint`, policy compliance validation, IaC security scan, and `what-if` drift review; any failed gate blocks deployment. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-114 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Secrets & Identity) | Must all pipelines use workload identity federation and managed identities only (no client secrets), and what exceptions are allowed? | Yes. Use workload identity federation and managed identities only; client secrets are prohibited by default and allowed only via time-bound security waiver with compensating controls. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-115 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Secret Handling) | What is the hard rule for secret delivery (Key Vault references only, no secrets in parameter files), and how are violations prevented? | Secrets must be sourced from Key Vault or equivalent secret store; no plaintext secrets in Bicep/parameters/repo; CI secret scanning and policy checks block violations. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-116 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Module Lifecycle) | What module/versioning strategy is required for Bicep modules (registry/template-spec usage, semantic versioning, deprecation policy)? | Use private Bicep registry with semantic versioning; consumers must pin exact module versions; deprecations require migration guidance and a minimum 2-release grace period before removal. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-117 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Scope Model) | What are the enforced deployment boundaries across management group, subscription, and resource group scopes? | Management group scope is used for policy/guardrails, subscription scope for shared platform resources, and resource-group scope for workload deployments; cross-scope changes require platform-owner approval. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-118 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Naming & Metadata) | What mandatory naming convention and metadata/tag schema must every module enforce beyond current minimum tags? | Enforce CAF-aligned naming plus required tags: environment, owner, costCenter, application, dataClassification, criticality, and managedBy; deployment fails when required metadata is missing. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-119 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Drift Control) | How often must drift detection run, what is the remediation SLA, and who approves/executes reconciliation? | Run automated drift detection daily and before every production deployment; remediate critical drift within 24 hours and non-critical within 5 business days, approved by service owner. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-120 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Break-Glass Governance) | What controls govern break-glass actions (authorized roles, time limits, audit evidence, mandatory IaC back-port timeline)? | Break-glass access is limited to named on-call/platform roles, max 24-hour elevation, ticketed audit evidence required, and mandatory IaC back-port within 1 business day. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-121 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Reliability Targets) | What explicit SLO/SLA, RTO, and RPO targets are required per workload tier? | Define tiers: Tier 1 SLO 99.9%, RTO <= 1 hour, RPO <= 15 minutes; Tier 2 SLO 99.5%, RTO <= 4 hours, RPO <= 1 hour; Tier 3 SLO 99.0%, RTO <= 24 hours, RPO <= 24 hours. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-122 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Backup & Restore) | Which resources require backups, what retention is required, and how frequently must restore tests be executed and evidenced? | All stateful data services require automated backups with policy-based retention (min 35 days operational plus long-term as required); perform documented restore tests at least quarterly. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-123 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Observability Baseline) | Which mandatory alerts, action groups, telemetry categories, and retention periods must be deployed by default via Bicep? | Deploy diagnostic settings for all supported resource logs/metrics to central Log Analytics, with Sev1/Sev2 action groups and default retention of 30 days hot plus 365 days archive. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-124 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Cost Guardrails) | Which mandatory cost controls apply (budgets, anomaly alerts, non-prod schedules, SKU caps), and who can approve overrides? | Require budgets with 50/80/100% alerts, non-prod auto-shutdown schedules, and approved SKU allowlists; override approval requires Engineering Manager plus FinOps owner. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-125 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Performance Baselines) | Which performance/capacity baselines (autoscale min/max, quota reservations, perf test triggers) are mandatory before production promotion? | Define per-workload autoscale min/max and quota reservations; run performance validation before production and block promotion on unexplained p95 latency regression above 10%. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-126 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Policy Exemptions) | What policy exemption workflow is allowed (owner, business justification, expiry, review cadence, and auto-expiry behavior)? | Exemptions require named owner, business justification, compensating controls, and max 90-day expiry; monthly review is mandatory and expired exemptions are auto-revoked. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-127 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Decommissioning) | What IaC-driven decommissioning standard is required for archival, retention, data destruction, and evidence collection? | Decommission via approved IaC change only, with retention/legal-hold check, required backup/export where applicable, secure data destruction, and stored evidence of completion. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-128 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Operations Ownership) | What is the required runbook/on-call ownership model for incident response and post-incident action tracking? | Each service must have primary and secondary owners, on-call rota, and versioned runbooks; Sev1/Sev2 incidents require postmortem and tracked corrective actions with due dates. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-129 | LOW | Phase 2 (Promotion Strategy) | Is deployment promotion required via immutable artifacts from Dev to Test to Prod, or are direct environment deployments acceptable? | Promotion must use immutable artifacts across Dev, Test, and Prod; direct environment rebuilds are not allowed for production deployments. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-130 | LOW | Phase 2 (Sustainability) | Should Azure Well-Architected sustainability guidance be mandatory for this project, and if yes, which measurable controls apply? | Yes. Apply sustainability guardrails: right-sized SKUs, non-prod auto-shutdown, storage lifecycle policies, and quarterly review of carbon/cost optimization opportunities. | 2026-03-07 |
